title: Braya involucrata
taxonomy: Braya involucrata is a species of flowering plant in the family Brassicaceae. It is native to the alpine regions of North America. The plant is classified in the genus Braya, which contains five species of flowering plants. Braya involucrata is a perennial herb that grows in rocky outcrops and alpine meadows. The species is also known as the mountain sandwort.
uses: Braya involucrata is a plant species native to the Arctic regions, but it also has some reported medicinal uses. The plant has been traditionally used to treat various health issues, including fever and respiratory problems. intro: Braya involucrata, also known as Arctic Alpine or Arctic braya, is a perennial plant species belonging to the Brassicaceae family. It has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, with reported health benefits including anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects, as well as potential applications in treating wounds and skin conditions. The bioactive constituents of Braya involucrata include glucosinolates, flavonoids, and phenolic acids, which contribute to its medicinal properties. In traditional herbal medicine, Braya involucrata is often used to make infusions, tinctures, and salves, which are used to treat various health conditions, including skin irritations, wounds, and respiratory issues.
